message.content:
Deep forward-looking analysis on "Yash Highvoltage Limited" (BSE SME 544310), Indian high-voltage transformer-bushing maker. Today is June 2026.

I want the forward outlook, catalysts, and management guidance. Pull from recent results commentary, scanx, T&D India management interviews, Groww, concall summaries if available.

Investigate and report, with sources and dates:
1. Management's stated guidance/targets for FY27 and beyond — revenue, margins, capacity, order book.
2. The ~₹400 Cr order book — composition, execution timeline, and visibility it provides.
3. The 550 kV greenfield plant — capacity addition, revenue potential, commissioning/commercial-production timeline (Q1/Q2 FY27 trial, H2 FY27 commercial?), and risks to the schedule.
4. International expansion: Yash Highvoltage USA Inc, the Weidmann Europe/N.Africa distribution deal, and export revenue ambitions — how material could exports become?
5. The Sukrut Electric 50% JV with Quality Power — strategic rationale and contribution.
6. Near-term catalysts (next 6-12 months) and the key things that could go wrong (de-rating triggers).
7. Any consensus/analyst forward estimates for FY27/FY28 earnings.

Give a clear, well-cited synthesis of the forward setup — upside drivers and downside risks. Findings only, no explicit buy/sell. Cite sources and dates.

content.text: I'll research Yash Highvoltage Limited's forward outlook. Let me start with several parallel searches.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about this business deal:

## Yash Highvoltage and Weidmann Partnership

Yash Highvoltage Ltd (Yash®) and Weidmann Electrical Technology AG (Weidmann) have announced the conclusion of an agency and distribution agreement. The 48-month agency agreement is effective from January 1, 2025.

### Scope of the Deal

Under this agreement, Weidmann will represent Yash in select European and North African markets for Oil Impregnated Paper (OIP) bushings up to 245 kV and High Current bushings up to 25 kA. The agreement extends Weidmann's representation to key markets including Germany, Spain, France, Belgium, Netherlands, Luxembourg, Portugal, Morocco, Algeria, and Tunisia.

### Company Expansion Context

During FY 24-25, Yash achieved new milestones by incorporation of a USA based subsidiary - "Yash HV USA Inc." and hiring of full-time business development manager based in the USA to expand sales footprint across America. The company's exports have grown at a C.A.G.R. of 52.8% over the past 3 years.

Based on the web search results, here's the information about Yash Highvoltage's Q4 FY26 results and commentary:

## FY26 Financial Performance

Yash Highvoltage reported audited consolidated results for the year ended 31 March 2026, with total revenue rising to Rs 2,351 million, an increase of 57 per cent year on year. Earnings before interest, depreciation and tax stood at Rs 604 million, with an annual EBITDA margin of 25.7 per cent. Net profit for FY26 rose to Rs 374 million, representing growth of 75 per cent and a PAT margin of 15.9 per cent.

## H2 FY26 Performance

In the second half of FY26 the company recorded revenue of Rs 1,355 million, up 46 per cent from Rs 925 million in the corresponding period. EBITDA for H2 reached Rs 372 million with a margin of 27.4 per cent, while net profit for the half year was Rs 237 million and the PAT margin was 17.5 per cent.

## Order Book

The order book stands at ₹400 Crore.

## Operational Highlights

The company entered the final phase of execution for its greenfield RIP/RIS manufacturing facility, with trial production expected in H1 FY27. Sales in FY26 reached a record 7,272 units compared to 5,752 units in the prior year, and the group operationalised Yash HV USA Inc while expanding distribution partnerships across Europe and the UK.

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding Yash Highvoltage Limited's FY27 guidance, order book, 550 kV plant, and management insights:

## FY27 Guidance & Growth Targets

Yash Highvoltage Limited is aiming for strong growth over the next five years, with the company's management expecting revenue to grow by 300 percent to 400 percent, or about 3 to 4 times the current level, targeting an average growth rate of around 30 percent per year. The company expects a significant portion of its growth to begin after FY27, when its new manufacturing plant receives approvals and starts operations.

## Order Book

The company has a ₹400 crore+ order book, reflecting strong demand for its products.

## 550 kV Plant & Expansion

The greenfield expansion project is in its final stage, with civil work substantially complete and commercial production targeted for H2 FY27, with the facility designed to manufacture Resin Impregnated Paper (RIP) bushings up to 550 kV, reducing import dependency. The greenfield project is expected to add around 6,000 units annually, with products engineered to international specifications for the Americas, Europe, and other markets.

## Investment & Capacity Details

The stated investment is Rs 153 crore, with commissioning targeted for H2 FY27. Yash Highvoltage expects the new facility to reach full capacity within three years, helping the company achieve a combined revenue potential of Rs. 550–700 crore.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Sukrut Electric, Yash Highvoltage, and Quality Power joint venture:

## Overview

Yash Highvoltage has acquired a 50% stake in Sukrut Electric Company, a global transformer component manufacturer, with the remaining 50% held by Quality Power. The joint venture was completed on January 9, 2026, with Yash Highvoltage acquiring a 50% equity stake in Sukrut Electric Company Private Limited for ₹5.24 crores.

## About the Companies

Sukrut Electric, established in 1967 and based in Pune, is a key supplier of transformer components to major Indian and global transformer OEMs. Quality Power specialises in high-voltage power products and power quality solutions, while Yash Highvoltage is a global supplier of condenser-graded bushings.

## Strategic Rationale

The strategic rationale is that Sukrut brings legacy manufacturing capability in transformer components, complementary product lines, and access to new customers and technologies. This strategic backward integration within the transformer component ecosystem is poised to drive significant revenue synergies over the coming years.

# Yash Highvoltage Greenfield 550 kV Plant Details

## Timeline
- **Trial Production**: Q2 FY 2026-27
- **Commercial Production**: H2 FY27 (second half of fiscal year 2027)
- **Commissioning Target**: End of Q1 FY 2026-27

## Project Status
The facility has completed civil construction with office building finished. "Plant and Machinery Procurement" is complete, and "Installation Process" is currently ongoing, according to the project status overview table.

## Product Specifications
The plant manufactures Resin Impregnated Paper (RIP) bushings up to 550 kV capacity. Management noted the company "expanded its design capability from 220 kV to 550 kV," reducing import dependency for these specialized electrical components.

## Investment & Financial Details
Capital expenditure totaled ₹59.20 crores on property, plant, and equipment during FY26. A board meeting was scheduled to consider "Fund Raising of Up to Rs. 150 Crore," though specific allocation to this facility wasn't detailed.

## Revenue Potential
No specific revenue projections or capacity figures (units/year) were disclosed in the articles. The company holds an order book exceeding ₹400 crores providing "execution visibility for the next 12 to 24 months," though facility-specific contribution wasn't quantified.

## Management Commentary
Operations focus on "operational stabilization to drive future growth" following recent acquisitions and international expansion through subsidiaries.

# Yash Highvoltage FY26: Key Details

## FY26 Financial Results
- **Revenue:** INR 235.1 crore (57% YoY growth)
- **EBITDA:** INR 60.4 crore (75% growth); margin 25.7%
- **PAT:** INR 37.4 crore (75% growth); margin 15.9%
- **H2 Performance:** Revenue INR 135.5 crore; PAT margin 17.5%

## Order Book
- **Size:** INR 400+ crore as of March 31, 2026
- **Comparison:** Up from INR 150 crore in March 2025
- **Visibility:** Provides 1-2 years execution visibility

## Product & Geographic Mix
- **Products:** RIP 83%, OIP 10%, high current 3%, others 4%
- **Geography:** Domestic 93%, exports 7% (vs. 95%/5% prior year)
- **Retrofit Services:** 6-7% of revenue

## 550 kV Greenfield RIP/RIS Facility
- **Location:** Vadodara, Gujarat
- **Investment:** INR 153 crore
- **Timeline:** 
  - Trial production: End Q2 FY27
  - Commercial production: H2 FY27 (October target)
  - Civil work and equipment substantially complete
- **Capacity Addition:** ~6,000 annual units
- **Strategic Benefits:** Import substitution, cost competitiveness, export unlock

## FY27 Management Guidance
- **Revenue/Invoicing Target:** INR 360-400 crore
- **EBITDA Margin:** 24-25% (maintained)
- **Order Booking Target:** INR 500+ crore
- **Growth Rate:** 40-45%
- **Export Goal:** 20%+ within 2-3 years

## Export Infrastructure
- **USA:** Established Yash HV USA Inc. sales/marketing office
- **Europe:** Distribution partnerships with Weidmann (select European/North American markets)
- **UK/Ireland:** Electrolink partnership

## Other Developments
- **Sukrut Electric:** 50% acquisition stake; FY26 revenue INR 25-26 crore; aspiration to reach INR 150-160 crore in 4-5 years
- **Potential Equity Raise:** INR 100-110 crore approved (of INR 150 crore authorization)
- **Debt-to-Equity:** 0.17x as of March 2026
- **Cyber Incident:** Reported; investigation ongoing; loss expensed

# Yash Highvoltage Greenfield Plant Analysis

## Timeline & Production Milestones
The article references a **transformer bushing facility** (not 550 kV specifically), with management stating: "trial production at its greenfield transformer bushings factory at Vadodara, Gujarat, in the first half of FY27, which is by September 2026. Commercial production is expected to begin in the second half (H2: October to March) of FY27."

## Capacity Expansion
The new facility will increase overall manufacturing capacity by approximately 6,000 units annually for RIP/RIS transformer bushings.

## Capital Investment
Capital expenditure is budgeted at approximately **Rs. 153 crore** across 2.5 lakh sq ft of land and 1.5 lakh sq ft built-up area.

## Order Book & Revenue Potential
As of March 31, 2026, the unexecuted order book reached **Rs. 400 crore**, up significantly from Rs. 150 crore a year prior. FY26 achieved record sales of 7,272 units with RIP bushings comprising 83% of value.

## Export Strategy
Management plans to "target the export market of high-value RIP/RIS transformer bushings from the upcoming Vadodara plant," marking a shift from current domestic-only production.

## Notable Gaps
The article contains **no FY27 guidance, specific revenue projections, or risk disclosures** from management.

# Yash Highvoltage Management Interview - Key Commentary

**Company:** Yash Highvoltage Ltd  
**Interviewee:** Keyur Shah, Chairman & Managing Director  
**Date:** December 25, 2025  
**Publication:** T&D India

## Demand Outlook

Shah indicates robust market conditions, stating: *"Demand is strong with rising global energy requirement due to rapidly rising data centre installations, urbanisation and industrialisation"* and identifies multiple growth drivers including EV infrastructure, railway electrification, and renewable expansion.

## RIP Bushings & Capacity

- **Current RIP capacity:** 3,000 units annually at existing Halol plant
- **Target:** Greenfield facility in Vadodara-Suncity to "materially increase RIP capacity" and expand export reach
- **Status:** Advanced construction stage with equipment commissioning forthcoming
- **Product range:** Up to 245kV RIP bushings (localized since 2016)

## Strategic Initiatives

Shah notes: *"With the greenfield RIP plant, testing facility upgrades, R&D investments, and Sukrut acquisition, we expect to strengthen market share."*

Key acquisition of Sukrut Electric (50% partnership with Quality Power Equipments) to access complementary products and customer base.

## Import Dependency

Company actively developing in-house RIP core production to reduce import reliance. Acknowledges domestic supply remains "relatively concentrated" but expects gradual import reduction with capacity expansion.

## Financial Performance

H1FY26 showed material revenue and profit improvements year-on-year, reflecting strong order inflows.

**Note:** No 550kV plant, USA exports detail, Weidmann comparison, or revenue/margin guidance provided in source material.

content.content:
# Weidmann-Yash Strategic Partnership: Key Details

**Effective Date & Duration:**
The agreement became effective in January 2025. The document does not specify a contract duration.

**Geographic Scope:**
Weidmann represents Yash in "select European and North African markets" including Germany, Spain, France, Belgium, Netherlands, Luxembourg, Portugal, Morocco, Algeria, and Tunisia.

**Products Covered:**
- Oil Impregnated Paper (OIP) bushings up to 245 kV
- High Current bushings up to 25 kA

**Strategic Significance:**
According to Matthias Freilinger, Head of Business Unit Europe Africa at Weidmann: "Offering our customers additional products through our supply chain not only creates value, but also enables Yash to bring their high-quality, competitive bushings into a new market at a time when demand is high, and supply chain is a challenge."

The partnership addresses two key industry challenges: extended lead times and increasing demand for high-voltage solutions. Yash brings an installed base of over 35,000 bushings globally across 60 countries, strengthening Weidmann's competitive position in the energy sector.

**Note:** The article does not specify an explicit contract duration period.

# Yash Highvoltage FY26 Key Metrics & Strategic Updates

## Financial Results (FY26 ended March 31, 2026)

**Full Year Performance:**
- Total revenue: Rs 2,351 million (57% YoY growth)
- EBITDA: Rs 604 million with 25.7% margin
- Net profit: Rs 374 million (75% growth) with 15.9% PAT margin
- EPS: Rs 13.08

**H2 FY26 Performance:**
- Revenue: Rs 1,355 million (46% growth vs H2 FY25's Rs 925 million)
- EBITDA: Rs 372 million at 27.4% margin
- Net profit: Rs 237 million with 17.5% PAT margin
- EPS: Rs 8.31 (vs Rs 6.76 prior year)

## Operational Highlights

**Manufacturing Expansion:**
"Entered the final phase of execution for its greenfield RIP/RIS manufacturing facility, with trial production expected in H1 FY27"

**Sales Performance:** Record 7,272 units sold (vs 5,752 units FY25)

**Technology & Acquisitions:**
- Completed acquisition of Sukrut Electric in partnership with Quality Power
- Successfully tested 245 kV oil-to-oil transformer bushing in-house

**International Expansion:**
- Operationalized Yash HV USA Inc
- Expanded distribution partnerships across Europe and UK

**Management Commentary:**
"FY26 as the company's strongest year in its two-decade history"

## FY26 Full Year Results

Yash Highvoltage Limited reported audited consolidated results for the year ended 31 March 2026, with total revenue rising to Rs 2,351 million, an increase of 57 per cent year on year. Earnings before interest, depreciation and tax stood at Rs 604 million, with an annual EBITDA margin of 25.7 per cent. Net profit for FY26 rose to Rs 374 million, representing growth of 75 per cent and a PAT margin of 15.9 per cent.

## H2 FY26 Performance (Q4 included)

In the second half of FY26 the company recorded revenue of Rs 1,355 million, up 46 per cent from Rs 925 million in the corresponding period. EBITDA for H2 reached Rs 372 million with a margin of 27.4 per cent, while net profit for the half year was Rs 237 million and the PAT margin was 17.5 per cent.

## Strategic Highlights

The managing director described FY26 as the company's strongest year in its two-decade history and attributed the performance to disciplined execution across domestic and export markets. He noted that strategic initiatives such as the greenfield expansion, recent acquisition activity and international partnerships have strengthened the long-term growth platform.

## Order Book and Outlook

The company's order book stands at over ₹400 crores, providing robust execution visibility for the next 12 to 24 months.

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about Yash Highvoltage's revenue potential and expansion plans:

Yash Highvoltage expects the new facility to reach full capacity within three years, helping the company achieve a combined revenue potential of Rs. 550–700 crore without needing major additional expansion.

The new plant, involving over Rs. 100 crore in investment, is expected to be ready for trial runs by early 2026 and begin commercial production in the second half of FY27.

Regarding exports, export markets are proving profitable, with price realizations 30–40% higher than domestic sales. This suggests strong export potential, though the search results do not specify the exact 20% export target you mentioned in your query.

Additionally, management highlighted a large and rising order book, progress on a greenfield backward integration project, and steps taken to widen the international footprint through a US subsidiary and distribution partnerships.

# Sukrut Electric Acquisition: Key Details

**Transaction Overview**
Quality Power Electrical Equipments Ltd (QPE) and Yash Highvoltage Ltd signed a Binding Term Sheet on July 9, 2025, to acquire 100% of Sukrut Electric Company Pvt. Ltd., a Pune-based manufacturer. The buyout is from a German multinational, with closure expected within two quarters pending regulatory approvals.

**What Sukrut Makes**
Sukrut Electric specializes in transformer components and serves hundreds of transformer OEMs globally.

**Strategic Rationale**
The partners cited transformer industry growth driven by transmission infrastructure upgrades, renewable energy integration, and grid expansion. Component supply constraints present a significant bottleneck. The combination aims to address global demand while expanding product depth and modernizing operations.

**Revenue & Figures**
No financial details or revenue figures were disclosed in the announcement.

**Backward Integration Logic**
The filing indicates this integration strengthens the supply chain for high-performance transformer components needed for grid modernization and energy transition projects.

**Quality Power's Role**
As a specialist in High Voltage Power Products and Power Quality solutions, Quality Power contributes expertise in scaling operations and serving evolving infrastructure demands.

**Key Management Quote**
Bharanidharan Pandyan, Joint Managing Director of Quality Power, stated: "Sukrut Electric is a legacy brand synonymous with trust, precision, and long-term relationships" across the transformer industry, positioning the combined entity to serve grid modernization needs effectively.

content.text:
I now have comprehensive, cross-checked coverage. Here is my synthesis.

---

# Yash Highvoltage Limited (BSE SME 544310) — Forward Outlook, June 2026

## Framing

This report covers the forward setup for Yash Highvoltage (YHV), an Indian condenser-graded transformer-bushing maker, as of mid-June 2026 — i.e., immediately after FY26 (year ended 31 March 2026) results. I synthesize management guidance, the order book, the 550 kV greenfield plant, international expansion, the Sukrut JV, catalysts/risks, and the (thin) analyst-estimate picture. Findings only — no buy/sell.

One framing caveat up front: this is a BSE-SME microcap (~₹2,100 Cr market cap, promoter-held 57.9%). "Guidance" here comes largely from management interviews and results commentary, **not** from formal broker models — formal sell-side consensus is essentially absent (see Section 7). Treat the management targets as aspirational management guidance, not independently modeled estimates.

---

## 1. Management guidance / targets — FY27 and beyond

**FY27 (near-term):**
- **Revenue/invoicing target: ₹360–400 Cr** (vs ₹235 Cr in FY26) — implying ~40–45% growth.
- **EBITDA margin guidance: 24–25%** (maintained roughly at FY26's 25.7%).
- **Order-booking target: ₹500+ Cr** of fresh orders during FY27 (vs the ₹400 Cr stock-of-orders at FY26-end).
- **Exports target: 20%+ of revenue within 2–3 years** (from ~7% in FY26).
([multibagg.ai](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))

**Medium/long-term (the "5-year" framing):**
- Management has publicly framed a **~300–400% revenue increase over ~5 years** (roughly 3–4x), i.e., **~30% CAGR**. ([tradebrains](https://tradebrains.in/high-growth-alert-transformer-stock-eyes-300-400-revenue-surge-over-the-next-5-years/) — note: this article was 403-blocked to me; the figures are corroborated via the search summary, but I could not read the full attribution.)
- A stated **combined revenue potential of ₹550–700 Cr** once the new Vadodara plant reaches full capacity (~3 years post-commissioning), "without needing major additional expansion." ([tradebrains "174% returns" piece](https://tradebrains.in/transformer-stock-that-delivered-174-returns-in-last-6-months-to-add-to-your-watchlist/))

**FY26 actuals (the base):** Revenue ₹235.1 Cr (+57% YoY); EBITDA ₹60.4 Cr (25.7% margin, +75%); PAT ₹37.4 Cr (15.9% margin, +75%); EPS ₹13.08; record 7,272 units sold (vs 5,752). H2 was notably stronger — 27.4% EBITDA margin. ([constructionworld](https://www.constructionworld.in/policy-updates-and-economic-news/yash-highvoltage-posts-record-fy26-consolidated-results/91679), [multibagg.ai](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))

> Confidence: FY26 actuals — high (multiple sources agree). FY27 guidance — medium (single detailed source, multibagg's concall summary; directionally consistent with T&D interviews). The ₹550–700 Cr "full-capacity" figure — medium-low (aggregator-sourced, not a hard management commitment with a fixed date).

---

## 2. The ~₹400 Cr order book

- **Size & trajectory:** ₹400+ Cr unexecuted order book as of 31 March 2026, **up from ₹150 Cr** a year earlier (roughly 2.7x growth). ([multibagg.ai](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883), [tndindia H1FY27 piece](https://www.tndindia.com/trial-production-at-yash-highvoltage-transformer-bushing-factory-expected-in-h1fy27/))
- **Visibility:** Management frames it as **12–24 months of execution visibility**. Against an FY27 invoicing target of ₹360–400 Cr, the book covers roughly a full year-plus of revenue.
- **Product composition of revenue (proxy for book mix):** RIP bushings 83% of value, OIP ~10%, high-current ~3%, others ~4%. Geographically FY26 was ~93% domestic / ~7% exports. Retrofit/retrofit-services run ~6–7% of revenue. ([multibagg.ai](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))

I could **not** find a granular breakdown of the ₹400 Cr book by customer, voltage class, or domestic-vs-export within the book itself — that detail does not appear in public commentary. The book is heavily RIP-weighted, consistent with management's stated "strong demand for RIP bushings" theme.

---

## 3. The 550 kV greenfield plant (Vadodara / Suncity)

This is the central forward catalyst.

| Item | Detail | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Location | Vadodara-Suncity, Gujarat (2.5 lakh sq ft land, 1.5 lakh sq ft built-up) | [tndindia](https://www.tndindia.com/trial-production-at-yash-highvoltage-transformer-bushing-factory-expected-in-h1fy27/) |
| Investment | **₹153 Cr** (capex ₹59.2 Cr already spent on PP&E in FY26) | [multibagg](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883), [scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144) |
| Product | RIP/RIS bushings **up to 550 kV** (vs current design capability to 220–245 kV) | scanx, multibagg |
| Capacity add | **~6,000 units/year** of RIP/RIS (vs current ~3,000 RIP units/yr at Halol) | [tndindia](https://www.tndindia.com/trial-production-at-yash-highvoltage-transformer-bushing-factory-expected-in-h1fy27/) |
| Trial production | **End Q2 FY27 (by ~Sep 2026)** | scanx, multibagg, tndindia |
| Commercial production | **H2 FY27 (~October 2026 onward)** | multibagg, constructionworld |
| Full capacity | ~3 years post-commissioning | tradebrains |

**Revenue potential:** The plate roughly doubles RIP capacity, and management ties the plant to the ₹550–700 Cr full-capacity revenue ambition. The plant is the vehicle for both **import substitution** (India's 400/765 kV bushings are largely imported today) and **export expansion** — management notes export realizations run **30–40% higher than domestic**, so the new high-voltage capacity is margin-accretive if export uptake materializes. ([tradebrains 174%](https://tradebrains.in/transformer-stock-that-delivered-174-returns-in-last-6-months-to-add-to-your-watchlist/))

**Schedule status (as of the latest commentary):** Civil work and office building complete; plant & machinery procurement complete; installation/commissioning ongoing, targeted to finish around end of Q1 FY27. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144))

**Risks to the schedule:**
- **Type-testing / customer qualification lag** — even after commercial production starts, 400–550 kV bushings require lengthy type tests and OEM/utility homologation; revenue ramp typically trails commissioning by quarters. This is the single biggest gap between "commercial production H2 FY27" and "revenue."
- **In-house RIP-core dependency** — management is still developing in-house RIP core production to cut import reliance; the CMD acknowledges domestic supply is "concentrated." Core supply is a real bottleneck. ([tndindia interview, 25 Dec 2025](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/))
- **Funding** — board approved a fund-raise of up to ₹150 Cr (≈₹100–110 Cr discussed); execution and dilution terms are not yet finalized. ([multibagg](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))

> Note a minor source inconsistency: trial production is variously described as "H1 FY27" (by Sep 2026) and "end Q2 FY27." These are consistent (Q2 = the back end of H1). Commercial production is consistently H2 FY27.

---

## 4. International expansion

- **Yash HV USA Inc.** — incorporated in FY24-25 and **operationalized in FY26**; a full-time US-based BD manager hired to build the Americas footprint. The 550 kV plant is explicitly engineered to international (Americas/Europe) specs. ([multibagg](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883), [tradebrains](https://tradebrains.in/yash-highvoltages-post-listing-surge-signals-a-high-voltage-growth-story/))
- **Weidmann (Switzerland) agency & distribution deal** — a **48-month** agency agreement **effective 1 January 2025**. Weidmann Electrical Technology AG represents Yash for **OIP bushings up to 245 kV and high-current bushings up to 25 kA** across **Germany, Spain, France, Belgium, Netherlands, Luxembourg, Portugal, Morocco, Algeria, Tunisia** (Europe + North Africa). Strategic logic: Yash gets channel access during a high-demand/long-lead-time period; Yash already has 35,000+ bushings installed across 60 countries. ([Transformer Magazine](https://transformer-magazine.com/tm-news/weidmann-and-yash-announce-strategic-partnership-in-european-and-north-african-markets/), [yashhv.com](https://www.yashhv.com/post/yash-and-weidmann-announce-strategic-partnership-in-european-and-north-african-markets))
- **UK/Ireland** — distribution via Electrolink. ([multibagg](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))

**How material could exports become?** Exports were ~7% of FY26 revenue but have grown at a ~52.8% CAGR over 3 years ([goodreturns directors' report](https://www.goodreturns.in/company/yash-highvoltage/director-report.html)). Management targets **20%+ of revenue within 2–3 years**. Given export realizations are 30–40% higher than domestic, hitting that mix would be disproportionately margin-positive. The realism of 20% hinges almost entirely on the 550 kV plant qualifying for export markets on schedule — so Sections 3 and 4 are tightly coupled. Confidence on the 20% target: medium-low (it's a stated ambition, dependent on plant ramp and overseas qualification).

---

## 5. Sukrut Electric — 50% JV with Quality Power

- **Structure:** YHV acquired **50% of Sukrut Electric Company Pvt Ltd for ₹5.24 Cr**; the other 50% held by **Quality Power Electrical Equipments**. Binding term sheet signed **9 July 2025**; deal **completed ~9 January 2026** (acquired from a German multinational seller). ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/orders-deals/yash-highvoltage-limited-completes-5-24-crore-acquisition-of-50-stake-in-sukrut-electric-company/29589926), [tndindia](https://www.tndindia.com/quality-power-and-yash-highvoltage-to-jointly-acquire-sukrut-electric/))
- **What Sukrut does:** Pune-based (est. 1967) maker of **transformer components**, supplying hundreds of Indian and global transformer OEMs.
- **Strategic rationale:** **Backward integration** into the transformer-component ecosystem — complementary product lines, new customer/technology access, and supply-chain security at a time when component supply is a sector bottleneck. CMD Keyur Shah lists it alongside the greenfield plant and R&D as a market-share lever. ([tndindia interview](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/))
- **Contribution:** Sukrut FY26 revenue ~₹25–26 Cr, with a management **aspiration to ₹150–160 Cr over 4–5 years**. At 50% and ₹5.24 Cr cost, it's small near-term but a cheap optionality/strategic toehold. ([multibagg](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))

---

## 6. Near-term catalysts (6–12 months) and de-rating triggers

**Upside catalysts:**
1. **550 kV plant milestones** — trial production by ~Q2 FY27 (Sep 2026) and commercial production H2 FY27 are the headline near-term proof-points.
2. **Order-inflow momentum** — progress toward the ₹500+ Cr FY27 booking target; any large 400/765 kV import-substitution wins.
3. **Export traction** — first material orders/realizations through the US subsidiary and Weidmann channel.
4. **Fund-raise clarity** — terms of the up-to-₹150 Cr raise (de-risks plant funding, but watch dilution).
5. **Sector tailwind** — strong global transformer demand from data centers, grid/renewables, EV and railway electrification (CMD's stated demand drivers, Dec 2025).
6. **Possible NSE migration / index inclusion** as the company scales beyond SME size (general SME-graduation catalyst; not yet confirmed).

**De-rating triggers / what could go wrong:**
1. **Plant slippage** — installation, type-testing, or qualification delays pushing commercial revenue beyond FY27; this is the highest-probability disappointment vector.
2. **Valuation/expectations risk** — the stock is up ~122% over 1 year and trades at **~54x trailing P/E** (₹735, ~₹2,102 Cr m-cap, P/B 11.4x). Much of the 5-year growth is already in the price; any execution wobble de-rates hard. ([screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))
3. **RIP-core / raw-material supply** — concentrated domestic supply and import dependence for cores.
4. **Margin pressure** — if export mix or pricing disappoints, the 24–25% EBITDA guide is at risk; margins already dipped at the PAT line in H1 vs H2.
5. **Cyber incident** — a reported cyber incident in FY26 (loss expensed, investigation ongoing) — minor financially but a governance/controls flag to monitor. ([multibagg](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883))
6. **Equity dilution** from the fund-raise.
7. **SME liquidity** — thin float, low institutional ownership (FII 0.05%, DII 11%), high volatility.

---

## 7. Consensus / analyst forward estimates — FY27/FY28

**There is effectively no published sell-side consensus.** I checked Trendlyne and Investing.com (the usual consensus aggregators for Indian equities) and **neither displays FY27/FY28 revenue, EPS, or target-price estimates** for YASHHV — Trendlyne shows "share price target of [blank] for Unknown Year." Screener likewise shows no forward estimates. ([Trendlyne](https://trendlyne.com/equity/consensus-estimates/2845096/13140063/yash-highvoltage-ltd/), [Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))

This is normal for a BSE-SME microcap — formal brokerage coverage hasn't initiated. The only forward numbers in circulation are **management's own**: ~₹360–400 Cr FY27 revenue, 24–25% EBITDA margin, and the ₹550–700 Cr full-capacity / 3–4x-in-5-years ambition. Anyone modeling FY27/FY28 EPS today is extrapolating from management guidance, not from independent analyst estimates.

---

## Bottom line on the forward setup

**Upside drivers:** A doubling of RIP capacity plus a brand-new 550 kV high-voltage line (commercial H2 FY27) positioned squarely at import substitution and higher-margin exports; an order book that has 2.7x'd to ₹400 Cr giving 12–24 months visibility; a structural sector tailwind (grid/data-center/renewables transformer demand); and credible international channels (Weidmann 48-month deal, US subsidiary). Management guides ~40–45% FY27 growth at ~25% EBITDA margins and a 3–4x revenue ambition over 5 years.

**Downside risks:** The whole thesis leans on one plant ramping on schedule and qualifying for export markets — type-testing and RIP-core supply are the realistic slip points. The stock already discounts a lot (~54x P/E, +122% in a year), so execution misses de-rate sharply. Add SME-specific risks (liquidity, possible dilution from the ₹150 Cr raise, a flagged cyber incident, near-zero institutional coverage).

**Evidence quality:** FY26 actuals and the plant/JV/Weidmann structural facts are well-corroborated across multiple sources. The forward numbers (FY27 guide, ₹550–700 Cr potential, 20% export target) rest mostly on management commentary relayed through a small number of outlets (multibagg's concall summary, T&D India interviews, ScanX, TradeBrains) — directionally consistent but not independently modeled, and the headline ₹400-Cr 5-year piece on TradeBrains was inaccessible to me directly (403). No formal analyst consensus exists to cross-check against.

---

### Sources
- [multibagg.ai — YHV FY26 results / concall summary](https://www.multibagg.ai/market-pulse/articles/yash-fy26-results-93883)
- [ConstructionWorld — YHV Posts Record FY26 Consolidated Results](https://www.constructionworld.in/policy-updates-and-economic-news/yash-highvoltage-posts-record-fy26-consolidated-results/91679)
- [ScanX — Advances expansion, targets trial production Q2 FY27](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144)
- [T&D India — Trial production expected H1FY27](https://www.tndindia.com/trial-production-at-yash-highvoltage-transformer-bushing-factory-expected-in-h1fy27/)
- [T&D India — "We see strong demand for RIP bushings" interview, 25 Dec 2025](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/)
- [T&D India — Quality Power & Yash to jointly acquire Sukrut Electric](https://www.tndindia.com/quality-power-and-yash-highvoltage-to-jointly-acquire-sukrut-electric/)
- [ScanX — YHV completes ₹5.24 Cr 50% Sukrut acquisition](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/orders-deals/yash-highvoltage-limited-completes-5-24-crore-acquisition-of-50-stake-in-sukrut-electric-company/29589926)
- [Transformer Magazine — Weidmann & Yash strategic partnership](https://transformer-magazine.com/tm-news/weidmann-and-yash-announce-strategic-partnership-in-european-and-north-african-markets/)
- [Yash Highvoltage — Weidmann partnership announcement](https://www.yashhv.com/post/yash-and-weidmann-announce-strategic-partnership-in-european-and-north-african-markets)
- [TradeBrains — Transformer stock eyes 300–400% revenue surge over 5 years](https://tradebrains.in/high-growth-alert-transformer-stock-eyes-300-400-revenue-surge-over-the-next-5-years/) (403-blocked to me; figures via search summary only)
- [TradeBrains — 174% returns / revenue potential ₹550–700 Cr](https://tradebrains.in/transformer-stock-that-delivered-174-returns-in-last-6-months-to-add-to-your-watchlist/)
- [TradeBrains — Post-listing surge / exports CAGR & US subsidiary](https://tradebrains.in/yash-highvoltages-post-listing-surge-signals-a-high-voltage-growth-story/)
- [Goodreturns — YHV Directors' Report (exports 52.8% CAGR, USA Inc.)](https://www.goodreturns.in/company/yash-highvoltage/director-report.html)
- [Screener — YHV valuation & financials](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/)
- [Trendlyne — YHV consensus estimates (no estimates published)](https://trendlyne.com/equity/consensus-estimates/2845096/13140063/yash-highvoltage-ltd/)
